data.body:
Operational remediation of the drift this workflow surfaced (2026-07-13).

FINDING (reproduced locally, terraform 1.10, frontier→896586841071): the daily terraform-drift sweep failed because of ONE real, pre-existing unapplied change — aws_instance.gerrit.user_data (676ec059→1cfc59bd). Root cause: PR #112 (2a4b552db, WS8 GitHub-OAuth, 2026-07-01) edited infra/terraform/user_data.sh; applies are manual, so the live instance attribute lagged the committed HCL. The drift workflow only landed 2026-07-11, so its first sweep surfaced the 10-day-old lag. NOT related to the auto-lander tf (ssm.tf / monitoring_autolander.tf): the plan showed 0 to add — every lander resource is already in state/applied.

The user_data delta is a 6-line COMMENT ONLY (documents why OAuth creds are fetched at compose-up, not cloud-init) — zero boot-behavior change. aws_instance.gerrit has no user_data_replace_on_change (default false) and ami is in ignore_changes, so the apply was a pure in-place attribute update: no instance replacement, no reboot, no re-run.

ACTION: terraform apply of exactly that plan (0 add / 1 change / 0 destroy). Same instance id i-00880b2c7f13527c5, EIP 52.3.128.230 unchanged, Gerrit HTTP 200 throughout. Fresh plan now: 'No changes' → the drift gate is green. Repo needed no change: origin/main HCL was already the correct source of truth; this only reconciled live AWS to it.
